  "findings": [
    "Belt mistracking due to off-center loading and idler skew",
    "Potential for material spillage and belt edge damage",
    "Exposed moving parts without adequate guarding",
    "Risk of entanglement and pinch points at idlers and pulleys"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop conveyor operation immediately",
    "Lockout/tagout power source before any adjustment",
    "Correct loading chute alignment to center material on belt",
    "Adjust skewed idlers to proper tracking alignment per CEMA standards",
    "Install or repair guards on all exposed moving parts per OSHA 1910.212",
    "Inspect belt and idlers for damage before restart"
  ],
  "rationale": "Belt mistracking from off-center loading and idler skew creates immediate hazards including material spillage, belt damage, and unguarded moving parts that pose entanglement and pinch-point risks. OSHA 1910.212 requires guarding of moving machine parts, and CEMA standards mandate proper belt alignment. Operation must stop until corrections are made.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(1) - Machine guarding",
    "OSHA 1910.212(a)(2) - Guarding of moving parts",
    "CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials - Belt tracking and idler alignment"
  ]
}
